description: Use when writing or improving a prompt, deciding how to phrase a request to Claude Code, structuring a non-trivial task (CONTEXT/TASK/CONSTRAINTS/EXAMPLES/OUTPUT), iterating after a wrong first output, or picking a high-leverage prompt pattern (orientation, challenge-me, small-fix, what's-broken, explain-it-to-me). Routes to chapter 09 (prompting) of claude-spine.

results.query: I keep getting bad output from this 'rewrite my landing page hero' prompt — Claude takes it in a generic direction. Help me restructure the prompt to get the voice I actually want.
this is a non-trivial task — adding multi-tenant teams to my B2B SaaS. Help me structure the prompt with CONTEXT / TASK / CONSTRAINTS / EXAMPLES / OUTPUT before I send it to Claude.
first attempt gave me boilerplate React. Iterate on the prompt — what did I leave out that would have pushed it toward production-quality?
which high-leverage prompt pattern fits this — I'm about to ask Claude what's broken in a flaky end-to-end test that fails 1 in 10 times
this CLI command prompt isn't matching properly in bash — fix the regex
design the welcome prompt UI for new users — what should it say
explain the difference between system prompts and user prompts in the Anthropic API
implement a command-line prompt that asks for confirmation before deleting files
the LLM is prompted with 8k tokens of context every request — is that hurting latency?
what's the right way to phrase 'I want you to audit my migration safety' so I get a real review and not just a checklist?
